evidence: All 5 experts cover evidence verification and fact-checking (axis 2) and claim classification/stance detection (axis 5) -- the two most central sub-domains
Rabbany's Veracity/CrediBench work and Hanselowski's multi-provider aggregation expertise provide good coverage of web search and retrieval (axis 1) and multi-provider aggregation (axis 4)
Atanasova and Hanselowski both provide deep coverage of confidence calibration and uncertainty quantification (axis 8)
Nakov's propaganda detection and media bias work complements source credibility and trust scoring (axis 3) alongside Rabbany's network-based credibility scoring
Rabbany's 6 canonical works span web retrieval, source credibility, evaluation methodology, and temporal graph methods -- the broadest single-expert coverage
gaps: MCP and agent runtime integration (axis 6) is thinly covered: only Hanselowski addresses agentic workflows, and no expert covers the MCP protocol specifically
Information extraction from web pages (axis 7) lacks a dedicated specialist; Augenstein's IE research and Hanselowski's argument mining provide partial coverage at best
Multilingual and cross-lingual concerns are mentioned by Nakov and Atanasova but not deeply operationalized in any expert's reasoning kernel
Production deployment, latency, and cost constraints are acknowledged in blind spots but no expert provides deep deployment-side knowledge
Adversarial robustness of credibility scoring and search result manipulation is a noted blind spot across multiple experts

evidence: Rabbany's profile contains 6 canonical works (richest among all experts), 8 signature ideas, a 6-item reasoning kernel per category, and detailed advantage_knowledge_base with 8 canonical concepts and 4 favorite benchmarks

Hanselowski's profile has the most granular reasoning kernel (5 items per category), 5 question playbook entries (most), and 6 preferred_evidence types with specific metric names (Recall@k, MRR, nDCG)
Atanasova's profile features 4 question playbook entries, 3 quote bank entries, and a detailed advantage_knowledge_base (6 canonical concepts, 5 favorite benchmarks, 4 known debates, 5 anti-patterns)

All 5 profiles include complete reasoning_kernel structures (core_questions, decision_rules, failure_taxonomy, preferred_abstractions), question_playbooks, quote_banks, critique_styles, blind_spots, and domain_relevance sections
Source confidence scores range from 0.75 to 1.0, with Augenstein having the highest (1.0) based on multiple Tier A sources
gaps: Augenstein's and Nakov's profiles are thinner: only 3 canonical works each, 2 question playbook entries each, and 1 quote bank entry each -- solid but not deep
Nakov's source_confidence is 0.75 with only 2 source refs (institutional profile + interview), limiting verifiable source alignment
Augenstein's quote bank has only 1 entry, and it is a paraphrase rather than a direct quote, reducing its evidentiary weight
No expert profile reaches the 'authoritative standalone reference work' level (23-25); all are in the 'rich profiles' to 'deep profiles' range
Blind spots are self-identified but not externally validated; some profiles may have undocumented gaps in coverage of emerging areas like RAG architecture patterns and MCP tool integration

weighted_median_calculation.note: Weights normalized from {reihaneh-rabbany: 0.3, isabelle-augenstein: 0.2, preslav-nakov: 0.24, pepa-atanasova: 0.24, andreas-hanselowski: 0.2} summing to 1.18

recommendations: BREADTH gap: Add an expert with deep MCP protocol and agent runtime integration expertise to cover axis 6
BREADTH gap: Add an expert specializing in information extraction from web pages (HTML parsing, structured data extraction, web table understanding) to cover axis 7
BREADTH gap: Strengthen multilingual coverage by expanding existing experts' profiles or adding a multilingual verification specialist
DEPTH gap: Enrich Augenstein's and Nakov's profiles with additional canonical works, more question playbook entries, and more quote bank entries
DEPTH gap: Add verified direct quotes from primary sources to profiles that currently rely on paraphrased material
DEPTH gap: Validate blind spots externally and add coverage of emerging areas (RAG patterns, MCP tool use) to relevant experts' reasoning kernels
